message:
feat(core,abi): port shadow line parser to core::pwd::shadow (bd-sh-epic)

Closes the parse/serialize asymmetry from bd-pgs-epic — that one added
format_shadow_line in core but the parser still lived inline as
parse_shadow_field + fill_shadow_entry in pwd_abi.rs (with
parse_shadow_field duplicated 18 times across two callers) plus a
separate inline parser in unistd_abi.rs::fgetspent / fgetspent_r.

New core surface:
  pub struct ShadowEntry { name, passwd, lstchg, min, max, warn,
                           inact, expire, flag }
  pub fn parse_shadow_numeric(s: &[u8]) -> i64
  pub fn parse_shadow_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<ShadowEntry>
  pub fn lookup_shadow_by_name(content, name) -> Option<ShadowEntry>

The parser uses two glibc-aligned sentinels:
  - empty/non-numeric signed field decodes to -1
  - missing/empty `flag` (u64) decodes to u64::MAX (~0UL)

That second one is a deliberate behavioral fix: the previous abi
inline parser used `0` for missing flag, which meant
`parse(format(spwd))` was not identity-preserving for the canonical
empty-flag case. With u64::MAX, the round trip is identity for both
sentinel shapes, and matches what real glibc emits.

abi changes:
  - pwd_abi.rs: parse_shadow_field deleted (was ~7 lines, called 18
    times); fill_shadow_entry now delegates to core. New
    fill_shadow_entry_caller helper packs into a caller-supplied
    buffer + writes the SpwdEntry numeric fields directly from
    ShadowEntry. getspnam_r and getspent_r each shrink ~40->~10 lines.
  - unistd_abi.rs: fgetspent and fgetspent_r each drop their ~36-line
    inline split-and-parse body in favor of a single
    parse_shadow_line call.

Verified:
  - 27 new core::pwd::shadow tests pass (numeric helpers, parse,
    lookup, parse-then-format round-trip identity for both sentinels
    and real-world lines)
  - 42/42 pwd_abi integration tests pass
  - 178/178 unistd_abi integration tests pass
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass

Closes bd-sh-epic.
